name: Willie Bobo
abstract: William Correa (February 28, 1934 – September 15, 1983), better known by his stage name Willie Bobo, was an American Latin jazz percussionist and jazz drummer of Puerto Rican descent. Bobo rejected the stereotypical expectations of Latino music and was noted for his versatility as an authentic Latin percussionist as well as a jazz drumme…

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: He popped into Birdland once or twice while I was playing there with Willie Bobo’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Well, the biggest attraction on the West Coast seems to be Willie Bobo. He works all the time! [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah? Well, ...(?)... how much he works. He's been here.

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: At the time he had Vince Guaraldi on piano, Al McKibbon on bass, Willie Bobo on regular drums, and Mongo Santamaria on conga drums.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997
